initiatives.name: Oklahomans for Equality (OKEQ)
initiatives.description: A statewide organization seeking equal rights for 2SLGBTQIA+ individuals and families through intersectional advocacy, education, and mutual aid programs. They operate the Dennis R. Neill Equality Center in Tulsa, providing health services, counseling, community spaces, and direct support while building networks of care and solidarity across Oklahoma.

initiatives.name: Trans Advocacy Oklahoma
initiatives.description: A statewide mutual aid and advocacy organization providing direct support, resources, and community care for transgender and gender-diverse Oklahomans. They coordinate emergency assistance, healthcare access support, and community building initiatives while advocating for trans rights and liberation.

initiatives.name: Freedom Oklahoma
initiatives.description: A statewide organization advocating for and providing mutual aid to Two Spirit, LGBTQ+, and gender-diverse communities across Oklahoma and within the 39 sovereign tribal nations. They coordinate community support, resource sharing, and advocacy initiatives while building power through collective action and creating spaces for community safety and thriving.

initiatives.name: Diversity Center of Oklahoma
initiatives.description: A statewide organization providing mutual aid, support services, and community resources for LGBTQ+ Oklahomans. They coordinate mental health support, social services, and community building initiatives while fostering inclusive spaces and networks of care across the state.

initiatives.name: Pantry Partners
initiatives.description: A statewide mutual aid network focused on addressing food insecurity across Oklahoma. They coordinate food distribution, community gardens, and resource sharing while building sustainable food systems and community resilience through collective action.

initiatives.name: OKHRA
initiatives.description: Oklahoma Harm Reduction Alliance, a grassroots harm reduction organization providing low barrier access to life saving supplies for people who use drugs (PWUD) in Oklahoma.
